Case study / 06Space data portal · Archived in 2025
Editorial + live space data

Beyond
Space

A consumer space-information hub combining news, NASA datasets, ISS tracking and launch intelligence in one responsive portal.

Scope
Frontend, server APIs and data aggregation
Role
Product and full-stack development
Period
2021—2025
Status
Shut down / live archive available
On this page
  1. The product 01
  2. Experience 02
  3. Architecture 03
  4. Lifecycle 04
01

Turn fragmented space signals into one useful destination.

Spaceflight stories, mission data and live tracking information are spread across specialist publications and public APIs. BeyondSpaceNews brought those streams together without flattening them into a generic feed.

The portal was positioned as a resource for spaceflight news, launches and astronomical discoveries. Its Nuxt application combined editorial feeds with public science data and vehicle information, while server routes normalized the very different upstream sources.

Editorial

News and blogs

A hero story and card-based feed surfaced current articles, with readable recency and direct links to original sources.

Science

NASA Explorer

Dedicated views exposed Astronomy Picture of the Day and other NASA datasets in an approachable format.

Live data

ISS positioning

An internal API route transformed current ISS position data into a user-facing tracking feature.

Reference

Vehicles and launches

Appwrite-backed collections and Space Devs data supported vehicle and launch-related pages.

02

A magazine surface with dashboard depth.

The visual system paired large editorial heroes with structured card grids. A sticky multi-section navigation and explore menu made the broader data portal understandable, while dark-mode support kept long reading sessions comfortable.

Feedback

Loading skeletons

Async views preserved page structure while server routes assembled content from caches or upstream APIs.

Recency

Human time

Time-ago components made fast-changing articles easier to scan than raw publication timestamps.

Navigation

Explore by domain

News, NASA data, tracking and vehicle content stayed distinct without feeling like separate products.

Sources

Open the original

External articles opened in new tabs, keeping attribution and the source reading experience intact.

03

A resilient data layer behind the magazine.

BeyondSpaceNews was not a static blog. Nuxt Nitro routes orchestrated several APIs, checked stored data first and returned normalized responses to the Vue interface.

The common pattern was Appwrite-first: an endpoint attempted to read cached or curated documents, then fell back to an upstream API when data was missing or Appwrite was unavailable. Space Devs requests also passed through a separate caching server to work responsibly within upstream rate limits.

Typical request flow / simplified
Nuxt 3 interfacePages + components
Nitro API routesNormalize requests
Appwrite + cache proxyStored data first
Space APIsLive fallback

Internal API surface

  • /api/blog/all
  • /api/iss/position
  • /api/nasa/mars-weather
  • /api/vehicles/:id

Technology and sources

  • Nuxt 3
  • Vue 3
  • TypeScript
  • Tailwind CSS
  • Nitro
  • Appwrite
  • NASA APIs
  • Open Notify
  • Spaceflight News API
  • The Space Devs
04

Four years of building around changing data.

The service was shut down in 2025, but its deployed archive still demonstrates the interface and breadth of the original product.

Engineering lesson

Cache the dependency

External APIs shape product reliability. Appwrite storage and the dedicated Space Devs cache reduced unnecessary upstream calls and made fallback behaviour explicit.

Product lesson

Unify, then specialize

A consistent shell can hold editorial, scientific and live data experiences without forcing them into the same presentation.

Archived deployment

The final site remains viewable at bsnnews.netlify.app.
